merger_name: MA Financial Group (MA Marina Fund) – Gold Coast City Marina

acquirers.name: MA MARINA FUND HOLDCO PTY LTD
MA MARINA FUND TT PTY LTD

targets.name: GOLD COAST CITY MARINA PTY LTD

other_parties.name: MA MARINA FUND OPCO NO. 1 PTY LTD

merger_description:
MA Marina Fund Holdco Pty Ltd and MA Marina Fund TT Pty Ltd (together, the **Acquirers**) propose to acquire 100% of the real property and business assets (both tangible and intangible) of the Gold Coast City Marina (**Gold Coast City Marina**). The Acquirers are indirectly solely controlled by funds managed by MA Financial Group Limited (**MA Financial**).

  

The Acquirers are part of the MA Marina Fund, managed by entities held by MA Financial, which invests in and operates marinas. The MA Marina Fund currently operates a portfolio of 16 marinas located along Australia’s eastern seaboard under the ‘d’Albora’ brand (**MA Marina Portfolio**). The MA Marina Portfolio includes 2 marinas in south-east Queensland – the Pacific Point Marina in Steiglitz on the Gold Coast, and the East Coast Marina in Manly, Brisbane.

Each marina in the MA Marina Portfolio operates as an integrated marina services facility combining vessel storage, maintenance and associated commercial uses. Marinas in the MA Marina Portfolio typically provide wet berthing and other ancillary marina services including dry storage, boatyard storage, marine workshop and industrial leasing, and marine fuel.

The Gold Coast City Marina is a full-service marina and marina industrial facility located in Coomera, Queensland. It provides wet berthing and other ancillary marina services including dry storage, boatyard storage, marine workshop and industrial leasing and marine fuel.

events.title: MA Financial Group - Gold Coast City Marina - Phase 1 determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Philip Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
MA Marina Fund Holdco Pty Ltd and MA Marina Fund TT Pty Ltd’s
(together, the Acquirers)’s proposed acquisition of 100% of the real
property and business assets (both tangible and intangible) of the
Gold Coast City Marina (Gold Coast City Marina) (the Acquisition).
The Acquirers are indirectly solely controlled by funds managed by
MA Financial Group Limited (MA Financial).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The Acquirers are part of the MA Marina Fund, managed by entities
held by MA Financial, which invests in and operates marinas. The
MA Marina Fund currently operates a portfolio of 16 marinas located
along Australia’s eastern seaboard under the ‘d’Albora’ brand (MA
Marina Portfolio). The MA Marina Portfolio includes 2 marinas in
south-east Queensland – the Pacific Point Marina in Steiglitz on the
Gold Coast, and the East Coast Marina in Manly, Brisbane.
Each marina in the MA Marina Portfolio operates as an integrated
marina services facility combining vessel storage, maintenance and
associated commercial uses. Marinas in the MA Marina Portfolio
typically provide wet berthing and other ancillary marina services
including dry storage, boatyard storage, marine workshop and
industrial leasing, and marine fuel.
The target, Gold Coast City Marina, is a full-service marina and
marina industrial facility located in Coomera, Queensland. It provides
wet berthing and other ancillary marina services including dry
storage, boatyard storage, marine workshop and industrial leasing
and marine fuel.

determination_table_content.item:
Overlap and relationship
between the parties
determination_table_content.details:
The Acquirers and MA Financial (together, the Parties) overlap in the
Gold Coast region in the supply of wet berthing and ancillary marina
services, including dry storage, boatyard storage, marine workshop,
marine fuel and industrial leasing (e.g. to boat builders, mechanics,
brokers), for recreational and commercial vessels.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form, information from third parties, and publicly
available information.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings:
• The Parties’ market share aggregation in either the supply of
wet berthing or ancillary marina services as a result of the
Acquisition is estimated to be low on any market definition;
• The Parties would continue to face competition from
multiple alternative suppliers of wet berthing and ancillary
marina services in the Gold Coast region, including The Boat
Works, Marina Mirage, Main Beach Superyacht Marina,
Southport Yacht Club, Hope Island Marina, Sanctuary Cove,
Runaway Bay Marina and Hope Harbour Marina.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.
